title: RunExampleInsights
description: PRIVACY-BOUNDED example-level insights for one optimization run, used by the terminal-first analytics surface to answer 'which examples are weak/unstable and what should I do about my dataset?'. This contract follows the same IP/privacy allowlist discipline as next_steps and example_score: by default (`safe_agent_projection`) it exposes only coarse counts, dataset-quality buckets, cohort-level impact labels, safe example references, and templated recommendations — NEVER raw proprietary example-scoring signal values, formulas, or raw prompt/response text. The `redactions` block declares what was withheld. `elevated` mode is owner-gated and backend-honored; a payload is only elevated if it echoes `privacy_mode: elevated`.

run_id.description: Identifier of the optimization run these example insights describe.

privacy_mode.description: Projection level applied to this payload. Defaults to safe_agent_projection (client-safe).

example_count.description: Total number of examples evaluated in the run.

weak_example_count.description: Count of examples flagged as weak (coarse count only).

unstable_example_count.description: Count of examples flagged as unstable across trials (coarse count only).

suspicious_example_count.description: Count of examples with suspicious review flags (coarse count only).

notable_example_count.description: Count of notable examples selected for bounded client review (coarse count only).

stable_example_count.description: Count of examples without weak, unstable, suspicious, or redundant review flags (coarse count only).

dataset_quality.description: Coarse dataset-quality bucket. Bucket only; the underlying signal is proprietary.

summary_text.description: Server-generated, redacted prose posture summary for this dataset. Opaque; contains NO internal scoring vocabulary, formulas, or raw values. Mirrors next_steps.posture.

example_rows.description: NOTABLE examples to review only (weak/unstable/suspicious/redundant) - NOT the full dataset and NOT a ranked ordering of all examples. Capped. Each row is cohort-of-one with safe, coarse, client-safe fields only; NEVER raw per-example scores.

safe_example_ref.description: Safe, opaque reference to one example (e.g. backend exref_ hash). NOT a raw id or prompt/response text.

review_priority.description: Review urgency derived from flags+difficulty. NOT a quality score or rank.

difficulty_bucket.description: Coarse difficulty bucket only; the underlying signal is proprietary.

suspicious_flags.description: Coarse issue labels only - no weights, thresholds, or values.

possible_cause.description: Templated, client-safe explanation. MUST NOT carry proprietary signal values, formulas, or thresholds.

cohorts.description: Groups of examples sharing a behavior, described at cohort level only.

count.description: Number of examples in this cohort.

impact.description: Templated, client-safe description of this cohort's impact on the run.

safe_example_refs.description: Safe, opaque references to representative examples in this cohort. NOT raw prompt/response text.

recommendation.description: Templated, client-safe recommended action for this cohort.

recommendations.description: Run-level dataset recommendations. May be empty when no safe recommendation is available.

reason.description: Templated, client-safe rationale. MUST NOT carry proprietary signal values.

redactions.description: Declares what this payload intentionally withholds, so consumers never assume omitted detail is absent rather than redacted.

raw_proprietary_signals_hidden.description: True when raw proprietary example-scoring signal values were withheld from this payload.

raw_prompt_text_hidden_by_default.description: True when raw prompt/response text was withheld by default under the active privacy mode.
